startup_description: Adversarial AI-powered startup idea validation that forces ChatGPT and Claude into opposing roles — one building the strongest case for your idea, one destroying it — producing an honest structured verdict before you waste months building the wrong thing.

hero_answers.Q1_problem_customer: Solo founders use AI as a yes-machine. A single AI model is optimized to be helpful, which means it agrees. There is no honest adversarial pressure-testing happening before they commit time and money. Forcing two AIs into opposing roles produces a qualitatively different and more honest output than any single model can deliver.
Q2_target_user: Technical solo founders aged 35-55 with a day job building something on the side
Non-technical solo founders with domain expertise but no co-founder to pressure-test with
Builders who already pay for ChatGPT and Claude and are familiar with the cut-paste validation pain
hero_answers.Q3_success_metric: Within 30 days: 25 paying users who each completed at least one paid analysis, at least 5 users who ran more than one analysis, and $500-1000 in revenue proving the transaction happens.
Q4_must_have_features: Single text input for plain-language idea submission
Claude in advocate role — strongest possible case for the idea
ChatGPT in adversary role — every reason the idea will fail
Synthesized structured verdict with strengths, fatal flaws, conflicts, open questions, and recommendation
Pay-per-analysis Stripe checkout with no account required
60-second or less turnaround from submission to report
Q5_non_goals: Building a full business plan generator
Market research or competitor analysis tool
Replacing the user's judgment — verdict is a recommendation not a guarantee

Q6_constraints.brand_positioning: Honest, adversarial, no bullshit — the anti yes-machine
Q6_constraints.scope: One idea in, one structured verdict out — nothing else in v1
Q6_constraints.economics: API costs covered by user payments from day one, no subsidizing early users
Q6_constraints.build_time: Completable by one person in nights and weekends in under 4 weeks
Q6_constraints.transparency: Must be honest that output is two AI models in structured roles, not a magic oracle

Q7_data_sources: OpenAI API — GPT-4o in adversary role
Anthropic API — Claude Sonnet in advocate role
No external market data sources required for MVP
Q8_integrations: Stripe — pay-per-analysis checkout, no subscription complexity in v1
OpenAI SDK — ChatGPT REST API integration
Anthropic SDK — Claude REST API integration
Simple Node.js or Python backend for orchestration and synthesis
Q9_risks: Adversarial output quality depends entirely on prompt engineering — requires iteration
API cost per analysis must stay under $0.40 to maintain viable margins at $5-10 price point
OpenAI or Anthropic building this natively into their own products closes the window
Bad analyses in low-training-data domains will damage trust early
Single person bandwidth — one production incident can kill momentum
hero_answers.Q10_shipping_preference: Start with a single-page app, no accounts, pay-per-analysis Stripe integration, and the two-model adversarial pipeline before adding any additional features, subscription logic, or user management.
